response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled polyglot individual contributor with genuine distributed-systems depth and broad full-stack experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for a CTO executive role. All positions on his resume are senior or staff IC roles — there is no evidence of building or scaling an engineering organization, managing senior technical leaders, interfacing with boards, or owning architectural domains at company scale. His agentic AI exposure is limited to one month at his current role (April 2025 to present) with vague framing around MCP tooling, and there is no demonstrated production ownership of multi-agent systems, LLM evaluation pipelines, or regulated fintech infrastructure at the depth this role demands.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async, microservices architecture
Current exposure to agentic AI tooling and MCP framework development at re:cinq signals awareness of the domain even if nascent
Broad technical range across backend, embedded, mobile, and frontend reduces blind spots in technology selection decisions
concerns: Zero executive or engineering-leadership experience — no CTO, VP Eng, or Director role anywhere in a 17-year career; all roles are individual contributor or solo-founder contexts
No evidence of building or scaling engineering teams, developing technical leadership layers, or governing hybrid delivery models — the core hard requirements of this position
Agentic AI and LLM depth is extremely thin: one month of current-role MCP work with no demonstrated production multi-agent system ownership, fine-tuning experience, or evaluation methodology

key_factors.factor: No team scaling or engineering org track record
